model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Michigan holds a slight edge in overall efficiency metrics, ranking 2nd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with elite defense and a top-10 offense, while Houston ranks 5th with a strong defense but a less potent attack. The Wolverines' balanced attack and defensive prowess should control the tempo and exploit Houston's offensive limitations, especially given Houston's recent loss to Arizona in the Big 12 title game and the physical toll of a tough tournament run. While Houston's elite defense gives them a credible upset path if they force turnovers and win the rebounding battle, Michigan's superior metrics, depth, and matchup advantage make them the more reliable pick to win the national championship.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Team Quality and Matchup Dynamics
reasoningStep.summary: Compare advanced metrics and matchup specifics for Houston and Michigan.
reasoningStep.evidence: Michigan ranks 2nd in KenPom and Torvik with elite defense (adjD 88.4/90.5) and top-10 offense (adjO 126.7/127.7). Houston ranks 5th with strong defense (adjD 91/91.9) but lower offensive efficiency (adjO 124.6/125.1). Michigan's defense is superior, ranking 2nd nationally, which should disrupt Houston's offense.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

First-round upsets are fun to predict, but under almost every scoring format, winning your pool is nearly impossible without identifying the team that cuts down the nets in April. There is no perfect formula for predicting who that team will be, but history does provide a few guideposts. Since the bracket expanded to 64 teams in 1985, every national champion has reached at least the semifinals of its conference tournament. modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Current game label: National championship
Round: Championship
